It is confirmed by the photos that the subject has a full basement.

The subject meets all FHA/HUD minimum guidelines as outlined by Handbook 4000.1 and the applicable Mortgagee Letter 09-28, except: Scrape & paint exterior window frames on enclosed porch, shed & back door frame. All paint chips should be removed from the area to ensure safety.

The appraisal is made "subject to" the repairs stated in the addendum of this report on the basis of a hypothetical condition that the repairs have been completed in a professional manner. The use of a hypothetical condition might affect assignment results.  USDA Required Repairs: 1. Replace broken exterior outlet cover on rear of attached garage.  2. Replace rotten window frame in rear of detached garage. 3. Replace missing soffit on rear of attached garage.  4. Replace missing garage ceiling tiles. 5. Finish painting interior stairway to basement and repair damaged drywall. 6. Repaint around doorway trim between living room and hallway. 7. Replace missing hallway smoke detector. 8. Replace missing light switch faceplate. 9. Replace missing light fixture in bedroom closet.  10. Repair damaged bedroom door. 11. Repaint drywall under bathroom window. 12. Replace missing cover in basement family room. 13. Replace missing trim in basement family room. 14. Scrape and repaint attached garage door trim. 15. Scrape and repaint chipping exterior window trims of those identified in the photos included in this report. 16. Scrape and repaint detatched garage door and trim. 17. Scrape and repaint detatched garage side door and trim. 18. All toilets to be in working order as stated in the contract.

This appraisal is made subject to the following repairs or alterations on the basis of a hypothetical condition that the repairs or alterations have been completed in a professional manner including: new flooring, interior paint, drywall repair, granite kitchen countertops, closet shelving, light fixture replacement, new blinds, and deck wash/stain. The use of a hypothetical condition might affect assignment results.

Subject to the following repairs or alterations on the basis of a hypothetical condition that the following repairs or alterations have been completed in a professional manner: updates include the replacement of the roof and removal of the overhang, along with the installation of new gutters. The entire electrical system has been replaced, and a new heating system has been installed. Plumbing updates include a new water heater, updated water and drain lines for the dishwasher, and replacement of various valves and drains. The kitchen and bathroom have been fully renovated, with the kitchen featuring new appliances and cabinetry, and the bathroom updated with new fixtures, retaining only the original tub. Both the interior and exterior have been repainted. Original hardwood floors have been refinished, and new flooring has been installed in the hallway, kitchen, and bathroom. Structural improvements include the replacement of the garage door and reinforcement of the roof structure and support posts. Landscaping has also been addressed through donated labor. The use of a hypothetical condition might afftect assignment results.

Subject to the completion of the following planned renovations and improvements: roof repair on backside of roof, pressure wash entire house, basic landscaping, paint garage door, shutters, and front door, replace deck, backyard landscaping, repair fence, replace all broken windows, renovate bedroom #1 and mold mitigation, renovate bathroom #1 (shower, vanity, flooring, and fixtures), removal of wall between kitchen and living room, new kitchen cabinets, countertops, and appliances, new flooring on first floor, walls repainted, new carpet and paint in basement, bathroom #2 and basement bathroom updated, paint interior of garage, and potential HVAC replacement. A hypothetical condition has been applied to the subject property that all of the previously stated improvements are compeleted.

Clarification of Intended Use and Intended User: First State Bank  The Intended User of this appraisal report is the Lender/Client. The Intended Use is to evaluate the property that is the subject of this appraisal for a mortgage finance transaction, subject to the stated Scope of Work, purpose of the appraisal, reporting requirements of this appraisal report form, and Definition of Market Value. No additional Intended Users are identified by the appraiser.  Effective January 1st, 2010, the ETHICS RULE now requires an appraiser to disclose any prior services provided regarding the subject property within the three years preceding the new request / assignment. The appraiser has not appraised the property in the past three years preceeding this request.   This appraisal report adheres to the development and reporting options of The Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP), FIRREA Title XI Appraisal Regulations and The Appraisal Independence Standards set forth by H.R 4173.  Geographical Competency Statement: This appraiser has geographic competency. The appraiser has prepared the appraisal in a familiar location and has spent sufficient time to understand the nuances of the local market and the supply and demand factors relating to the specific property type and the location involved.  Section 1455.245  Scope of Property Condition Inspections by Real Estate Appraisers   Licensed real estate appraisers may include in an appraisal report comments on the condition of the property that affect property value, including physical deficiencies, adverse conditions and renovations, based on observations by the licensed real estate appraiser during the appraisal of the property. Licensed real estate appraisers may not provide these comments for compensation, or with the expectation of receiving compensation, directly or indirectly, as a substitute for a home inspection, or a home inspection report, prepared by a licensed home inspector. All appraisal reports of a property that is 1 to 4 residential units prepared by licensed real estate appraisers must include the following statement: "The comments by the licensed real estate appraiser contained within this appraisal report on the condition of the property do not address "standards of practice" as defined in the Home Inspector License Act [225 ILCS 441] and 68 Ill. Adm. Code 1410 and are not to be considered a home inspection or home inspection report."  ILLINOIS FEE DISCLOSURE: To comply with Illinois state regulations, when an AMC engages an appraiser for an assignment, the appraiser must prominently display the appraisal fee received from the AMC in the certification as follows:   The compensation for this appraisal assignment is $475.00.  The appraiser has adhered to ANSI standards in measuring the subject property.
